title: Istio mTLS Modes
description:
## Istio mTLS Modes

Istio uses `PeerAuthentication` to configure mutual TLS (mTLS) between services. There are two relevant modes: `STRICT` and `PERMISSIVE`.

You have a legacy service (no sidecar) that needs to call a new mesh-enrolled service. Which mTLS mode should you set on the **destination** service to allow both plain-text and mTLS traffic simultaneously during migration?

options.text: STRICT — enforces mTLS for all inbound traffic
PERMISSIVE — accepts both mTLS and plain-text traffic
AUTO — automatically negotiates based on client capability

explanation: PERMISSIVE mode allows the proxy to accept both mTLS-encrypted traffic (from sidecars) and plain-text traffic (from non-mesh clients). This is the standard migration strategy: set the destination to PERMISSIVE, onboard all clients into the mesh, then switch to STRICT. STRICT rejects plain-text. DISABLE turns mTLS off completely. AUTO is not a valid Istio PeerAuthentication mode.

hints.title: PeerAuthentication resource
hints.body: Apply a PeerAuthentication in the destination namespace with mtls.mode: PERMISSIVE to allow both traffic types.
